title: Sons of Gul'dan
short_description: orc warlock-kin who carry gul'dan's sigil in orgrimmar's shadow-rooms
content: A black-banner cabal that takes its name from the warlock who first led the orcish clans into the Burning Legion's hand. Their officers wear his sigil openly — a hooded shaman's mask scored with three claw-marks — and gather in the smoke-rooms of Orgrimmar's Cleft of Shadow rather than the open Drag. Their warlocks bind succubi and voidwalkers without apology, and they keep no oath to Thrall's shamans. Most of the Horde will not drink with them, but few are foolish enough to say so within earshot.

title: Acherus Vanguard
short_description: ebon blade death knights who ride the ruined roads of lordaeron seeking lost fellows
content: A company of death knights freed at the breaking of Light's Hope Chapel, who chose, having no living tribe left to return to, to keep the name of the necropolis they were forged in. They ride the old roads of Lordaeron — Tirisfal, Andorhal's dead villages, the Bulwark — as a recovery column, retrieving fellows the Ebon Blade has not yet found and putting down those too far gone. Their captain is a tauren whose first death came in the Grimtotem raids on Mulgore; raised at Acherus, broken from Arthas's hand at Light's Hope, he says little, and what he says is heard. Their banner is the Ebon Blade's sigil over a black bull's skull.

title: Stormwind Vanguard
short_description: stormwind veterans sworn to the king who hold the city and ride with si:7
content: Veteran swordsmen of the city's standing army — sons and grandsons of the men who held Stormwind Keep through the First War and rebuilt it after Lothar fell at Blackrock. Their elder captains marched through the second Dark Portal into Outland under Khadgar's banner; the younger ones cut their teeth in the Defias's long shadow over Westfall. They train in the Old Town barracks when home and ride with the SI:7 columns when not, answering through High Marshal Twinbraid to the king directly. New recruits drill six weeks before they are given a tabard. The Vanguard takes no warlocks and no oath-breakers; what else a soldier brings, it does not ask.

title: Ashenvale Sentinels
short_description: kaldorei sentinel daughters who guard ashenvale's forest road against warsong scouts
content: Daughters and watchful sons of the Sentinel order who keep the forest road between Astranaar and the Zoram Strand. They answer to Shandris Feathermoon at Feathermoon Stronghold, though most have not seen her in a season; the orders that reach them come written in Darnassian on bark slips. They ride sabers, not horses, and they shoot first when Warsong scouts are sighted. New recruits are taken on a month's trial in the deep woods, and not all come back.

title: Knights of the Ebon Hold
short_description: dwarf death knights of the ebon blade garrisoning the necropolis above the plaguelands
content: Heavy cavalry of the Ebon Blade who keep the front near the Hold itself — the floating necropolis above the Eastern Plaguelands that Highlord Mograine claimed when the Lich King's chain broke. The Hold's dwarves are veterans of Ironforge's mountain regiments, dead a season or two, riding skeletal chargers now in plate that no smith in Khaz Modan ever forged. They speak less than living dwarves, drink as much, and pay for their own ale. Their grandmaster sends them where the Argent Crusade will not.

title: Stalkers of Teldrassil
short_description: kaldorei rangers who patrol teldrassil's boughs and hunt satyrs in elune's name
content: A younger company than the Sentinels — drawn from the kaldorei who came of age after Teldrassil's growing, and who never knew the long sleep. They patrol the World Tree's lower boughs and the Wellspring shores, hunting satyrs and night-saber poachers, and they take ranger initiates as young as fifty winters. Their tabard bears the moon-and-crescent over a single white feather. Word among the Darnassian guards is that they answer to a captain who was a priest of Elune before she put her stole away and took up the bow.

title: Darkspear Shadowstalkers
short_description: darkspear shadow-hunters bound to vol'jin who slip unseen where grunts cannot go
content: Shadow-hunters and quiet blades of the Darkspear tribe — Sen'jin's hidden hand. They are bound by old oaths to the chieftain, by newer ones to Vol'jin, and by the long memory of when the Darkspear lost their first home in the Stranglethorn Vale to the Gurubashi and the murlocs. They take work that the Warchief's grunts cannot — slipping into Theramore's outer farms, the old Zul'Gurub ruins, and the deeper haunts where Amani trolls still hold against the sin'dorei. Their initiates are marked by a small bone-needle tattoo above the right eye. They share fire and rum with the Echo Isles' own; they trust no orc, and few of their own.

title: Argent Vigil
short_description: argent crusade knights of light who stand watch at light's hope chapel
content: A standing company of the Argent Crusade pledged at Light's Hope Chapel — where the Lich King's own butchers fell to the Light in the same hour, and Tirion Fordring lifted the Ashbringer reforged. Their elder knights served the old Argent Dawn through the long quiet years of cleansing the Plaguelands acre by acre, and a few still remember Uther's banner before Stratholme. They wear the Crusade's gold-and-white openly, and answer to Highlord Tirion Fordring through Lord Maxwell Tyrosus. Their roster takes any race or rite — a Forsaken in the field beside a Stormwind footman — so long as the oath is given and held. They sing the Light's evening hymn before each muster, and not always in Common.

title: Daughters of Cenarius
short_description: kaldorei druidesses sworn to cenarius who walk the deep dream and mend felwood's wounds
content: A circle of kaldorei druidesses sworn to the demigod-stag of the Moonglade. They walk the long path of barkskin — leaf into hide, hide into branch, branch into the deeper dream — and they answer to the elder druids of the Cenarion Enclave in Darnassus, though many have not slept in a moonwell for years. Their eldest fought beside Malfurion at the breaking of Nordrassil when Archimonde climbed Hyjal; the younger ones now mend the felwood-poisoned glens that the Burning Legion left behind. None of them take a tabard willingly; the sigil is a green stag-head, and it shows only when they wish.

title: The Violet Eye
short_description: arcane scholars charged by khadgar to watch the tower of medivh and name what walks there
content: A scholarly order founded by the archmage Khadgar in the years after Karazhan fell silent, charged with watching the tower of Medivh and naming what walks there. The Eye's members carry a small violet glass charm and answer to no court directly. Many are mages; some, more quietly, are warlocks who learned to keep their company welcome only by leaving most of what they study unspoken. They send couriers between Stormwind and Dalaran on horseback rather than by portal, on the principle that what a mage opens, a demon may follow through.

title: The Deathstalker Cabal
short_description: forsaken shadow-blades answering to sylvanas who braid venom-craft and dark sorcery
content: A black-cloak company drawn from the Royal Apothecary Society's outer ranks and the Deathstalker corps both — Forsaken who chose neither pure venom-work nor pure shadow-work but braided the two. Their senior officers were among the first to tear free of the Lich King's chain when Sylvanas broke his hold and led them down into Capital City's catacombs; some still bear the brands the Scourge put on their bones. They report up through the Undercity to the Banshee Queen's chair directly, by way of channels few are told the names of. They keep their faces hooded in Tirisfal's open daylight not from shame but from old habit. The Cabal welcomes warlocks the Royal Apothecaries find too erratic, and apothecaries the warlocks find too cautious.

title: Aldor Lightbearers
short_description: draenei priests of the aldor who mend the wounded and carry the light through each exile
content: Priests of the Aldor in their third exile — first from Argus when the eredar gave themselves to Sargeras, then from Draenor when the orcs of the Legion's whisper broke their cities, now from Shattrath itself when Kael'thas's blood elves seized the Tempest Keep. They are sworn to A'dal of Shattrath, but A'dal speaks in song too rare for many to hear, and the company's senior anchorite — a draenei matriarch out of the Exodar — speaks for him. They mend the wounded in field tents that smell of incense and seared bone, and they refuse no soldier the Light's word, including the dying who whisper for it in tongues she does not know.

title: Keepers of Moonglade
short_description: druid wardens of nighthaven who tend the moonwell and guard moonglade's ancient quiet
content: A wardenship of druids living in the long quiet of the Moonglade itself, between Felwood's blight and Winterspring's snow. They tend the moonwell at Nighthaven and walk the dreamways under Keeper Remulos's care. They take in tauren druids of the Cenarion Circle as readily as their own kaldorei kin; the war on the surface is not their first concern. When they ride out, it is because a corruption has reached past the glade's wards — and then they ride with claw and root, and Felwood remembers them.

title: Magisters of Silvermoon
short_description: sin'dorei arcanists who kept silvermoon's magister-houses alive after kael'thas led others to ruin
content: A circle of arcanists drawn from Silvermoon's surviving magister-houses — those who did not follow Kael'thas Sunstrider into Outland's ruin. They keep apartments in the Royal Exchange and laboratories in the Court of the Sun, and most have lectured at least one season at the Magisters' Terrace. Their senior — an old half-blind woman who served the Sunstrider line before the Scourge came up the Dead Scar — chooses initiates herself, and refuses more often than she accepts. They wear red and gold, and they do not forgive Arthas easily.

title: Acolytes of Acherus
short_description: newly-raised ebon blade initiates who drill acherus's grounds and hold death's breach
content: Newer dead than the Vanguard — initiates of the Ebon Hold who took up the rune-blade in the past season and still answer to the masters above. They drill the dueling-grounds of Acherus when the Hold is not moving, and they bunker at Death's Breach when the Argent Crusade calls for line-troops. Most are dwarves out of Khaz Modan whose families have buried them already and would not welcome them home. They keep their old surnames anyway, as a courtesy to themselves.

title: The Emerald Wardens
short_description: kaldorei druids who walk the dream's outer paths and hold back the growing nightmare
content: Wardens of the Emerald Dream's outer paths — druids who spend half their lives asleep and the other half watching the waking world from the eyes of stag and owl. They walk the green-hued borders between Moonglade, Hyjal, and the deeper Dream. Of late, those borders have grown sour; the Nightmare presses where it should not, and Wardens come back with claw-marks no living beast left. Their order has no fortress, no banner, no song; you know them by the bark-rune at the wrist.

title: Sin'dorei Blood Knights
short_description: sin'dorei knights of the blood who hold the light by liadrin's example in silvermoon
content: The order founded by Lady Liadrin when Quel'Thalas needed paladins of its own and the Alliance would not lend them — sin'dorei knights who bent the Light by force to their hand, drawing on M'uru's broken radiance until A'dal's mercy let them keep it freely. They train at the Hall of Blood in Silvermoon under Master Belathis, and they march under Liadrin's banner. They are neither apologetic nor proud about the years of compulsion; they simply hold the Light now, and use it. Their colours are scarlet and gold.

title: Burning Blade Coven
short_description: orc warlocks of the old burning blade line who name mannoroth's fire without flinching
content: Inheritors of the old Burning Blade Clan — the orc warlock-line that worshipped fel-fire and the demon Mannoroth long before Gul'dan's Stormreavers rose. They keep to the Drag in Orgrimmar's lower terrace and to certain caves above Razor Hill that no orc patrol enters by choice. They bind imps and felhunters openly and speak of Mannoroth's name without flinching. Most of the Horde considers them a buried disgrace; they consider that the Horde has forgotten what made it strong.

title: Runetotem Earthwalkers
short_description: tauren druids who trace their line to hamuul runetotem and walk mulgore's long earth-paths
content: A druidic kinship out of Mulgore that traces its line back to Hamuul Runetotem, the first tauren druid taught at the Moonglade by Malfurion's own circle. They walk the long earth-paths between Thunder Bluff, Mulgore, and the Stonetalon Mountains, and they answer to the Cenarion Circle through Hamuul himself when he calls. Their elders walked Felwood and Ashenvale in the long mending after Archimonde's fall at Hyjal; the younger ones patrol the Cenarion Wall against the silithid stirring out of Silithus. They take in young druids of any tauren tribe — Bloodhoof, Skychaser, Wildmane — and a few sin'dorei, lately, on probation.

title: Bloodhoof Warband
short_description: tauren axemen sworn to cairne bloodhoof who have held mulgore against centaur and grimtotem alike
content: A warband sworn to Chieftain Cairne Bloodhoof of Thunder Bluff — heavy-axe veterans of the long Grimtotem feud, the centaur raids out of Desolace, and the Quilboar hostings out of the Razormane camps in the Barrens. They wear plain hide-and-bronze, no painted heraldry; the chief himself dislikes ornament. They drink at the Pools of Vision before each march and bury their dead under stone, not pyre. They take only tauren, save for one orc who saved an officer in a centaur ambush at the Crossroads, and was offered a tabard in the field and chose to keep it.

title: Frostwolf Honor Guard
short_description: frostwolf veterans who follow thrall as his standing escort and keep the old clan rites
content: The veteran axemen of the Frostwolf clan — those who followed Thrall through Lordaeron's prisons, the breaking of the orc clans on Kalimdor, and the founding of Orgrimmar. They serve as the Warchief's standing escort when he moves between Grommash Hold and the field, and they keep the Frostwolves' old mountain-rites against the day the clan returns north to Alterac. Their elders fought beside Drek'Thar at the Wetlands crossings; the younger ones cut their teeth in Alterac Valley's long stalemate. Their tabard bears a black wolf-head on iron-grey; they take no oath but to the Warchief and the clan.

title: Spiritbearers of Sen'jin
short_description: darkspear witch-doctors who carry the loa-rites sen'jin bore and speak to the wild gods
content: A troll religious order out of the Echo Isles — shadow-hunters and witch-doctors who carry the loa-rites that Sen'jin himself bore before he fell to the Sea Witch's serpents in the Vale. They speak to the wild gods at the old standing stones beneath Sen'jin Village and walk the long roads to Zul'Drak when the loa there grow restless. Their elders remember when the Darkspear lived in the Stranglethorn jungle; the younger ones have only known the village under Vol'jin's hand. They take troll shamans and priests as a matter of birth-right, and a few orcs who learned to drink the loa's cup without choking. Their tabard is bone-white on dark green.

title: Stormpike Wildcats
short_description: dwarven hunters of the stormpike clan who hold alterac valley's icy ridges against the frostwolves
content: A war-band drawn from the Stormpike clan's western lodges — dwarven hunters and gun-handed warriors who hold the icy ridges of Alterac Valley against Drek'Thar's Frostwolves. Their elders served under Vanndar Stormpike at the Field of Strife; the younger ones came down from Khaz Modan in their grandfathers' boots. They keep a forward camp at Frostwolf Pass and a winter hall at Dun Baldar's south rampart. They share strong drink with any Alliance soldier and answer to High Captain Justin Bartlett when the line breaks. Their tabard is white and storm-blue; their gun-stocks bear three notches each for an orc taken in trade.

title: SI:7 Black Daggers
short_description: stormwind's deniable blades who shadow defias remnants and slip forsaken agents from tirisfal farms
content: The deniable hand of Stormwind Intelligence — rogues, infiltrators, and quiet poisoners who answer to Master Mathias Shaw out of the Old Town safehouse. The Black Daggers take work the Vanguard's swordsmen cannot: shadowing Defias remnants in Westfall, slipping Forsaken agents out of Tirisfal's farms, and one job in Dalaran's Underbelly that no one is permitted to record. They take recruits from the human merchant houses, the night-elf assassin-school at Auberdine, and a few gnomes from Tinker Town with very particular gifts. Their tabard, when worn, shows a black dagger on a charcoal field; most of the time it isn't worn.

title: Argent Lance
short_description: argent crusade heavy cavalry who carry tirion fordring's banner where the footmen cannot ride
content: A heavy-cavalry order of paladins sworn to Highlord Tirion Fordring's banner — the mounted hand of the Argent Crusade that rides where its footmen cannot reach. Their captains served the Silver Hand before the order was broken at Stratholme; the younger knights were anointed at Light's Hope Chapel in the years since the Ashbringer's reforging. They keep a chapter house at Tyr's Hand and answer to Lord Maxwell Tyrosus when Tirion is afield. They take human and dwarf knights as a matter of course, and increasingly draenei vindicators who shed their Aldor stole for the lance. Their tabard is gold-and-silver-trimmed white.

title: Ironforge Mountainshields
short_description: bronzebeard dwarves who hold the ironforge gates and guard the deep galleries beneath the mountain
content: The standing infantry of the Ironforge Senate's military arm — warriors and paladins of the Bronzebeard line, with a handful of Wildhammer dwarves who came down from Aerie Peak when the Senate's call went out. They hold the road from Kharanos to the Ironforge gates and the deep galleries where the city's vaults touch the old Titan-works. Their elders fought through the Second War alongside the Bronzebeard king; their initiates drill the Hall of Arms under Senator Magni Bronzebeard's old standard. They take dwarves of any clan and gnomes who serve as their gun-smiths and explosives-handlers. Their tabard is bronze-and-rust on slate.

title: Reclaimers of Gnomeregan
short_description: gnomish engineers sworn to mekkatorque's campaign who press toward gnomeregan's irradiated halls
content: A gnomish company sworn to High Tinker Mekkatorque's reclamation campaign — engineers, mechanic-warriors, and a handful of mages who learned arcane from the same teachers who taught them to wire a turret. They keep a forward camp at New Tinkertown above the old city and answer to Mekkatorque's enemies through any one of three field captains. Their elders survived the irradiation that drove the gnomes from the city; the younger ones were born in Ironforge's refugee quarters and have not yet set foot inside Gnomeregan's outer halls. They take gnomes of every speciality and a few dwarves who can fit through the gallery doors. Their tabard is brass-and-copper on workshop-grey.

title: Earthen Ring Conclave
short_description: tauren and draenei shamans of the earthen ring who mend the elements no one else has time for
content: A neutral fellowship of shamans drawn from Mulgore and the Exodar — tauren elementalists who walked the long path with Hamuul Runetotem, and draenei vindicators who carried the Light's understanding of the elements out of Outland's ruin. They keep a small camp at Nighthaven and a second on the Maelstrom's outer rim, and they answer to the Earthen Ring's leadership through circle-elder Muln Earthfury when his counsel reaches them. They mend the seared earth where the Burning Legion has wounded it and listen to the elements no one else has time for. Their tabard is unstitched bronze on slate; they share fire and salt with any race that respects the bond.

title: Kirin Tor Magistrate
short_description: kirin tor working magisters who adjudicate arcane disputes beneath dalaran's violet citadel
content: A circle of arcanists serving the Kirin Tor's lesser council — Dalaran's working magisters, not its archmagi, but the ones who actually adjudicate arcane disputes when two members of the city's guilds bring grievance. They keep chambers in the Violet Citadel and offices in the Underbelly's quieter alcoves, and they answer ultimately to Rhonin's circle through Vereesa Windrunner when she still walks the council halls. Many took up scholarship under Antonidas at Dalaran's first founding; the younger members came of age in the city as it rose above Crystalsong Forest. They wear violet trimmed in silver, and their judgments are not appealable to crowns or chieftains.

title: The Stalwart Vigil
short_description: veterans of every front united against the lich king who drill under bolvar's old standard
content: A standing company of veterans from every front of the Lich King's war — the men and women who hold the line against the Scourge at Icecrown Citadel's outer barbicans. The Vigil takes its captains from any race or faction that has lost enough to the Lich King to swear the oath: a draenei vindicator beside a Forsaken deathstalker, a human paladin beside a tauren druid, a sin'dorei magister beside a Stormwind ranger. They answer to Highlord Bolvar Fordragon's old standard, kept now by Lady Jaina Proudmoore in her field tent above Crusaders' Pinnacle. They drill the Argent Tournament Grounds at sunset and march on the Citadel before each new moon. Their tabard is the Vigil's pale lantern on a black field.
